channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: The cloud giants stumble
item.description: Recent financial results from the major cloud providers reveal a concerning trend in cloud adoption and growth. AWS reported just 13% growth in the final quarter of 2023, Microsoft Azure saw 19% growth, and Google Cloud achieved 26% growth—all below market expectations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why the generative AI hype is good
item.description: Elon Musk predicts artificial general intelligence (AGI) by 2025. Former OpenAI employee Leopold Aschenbrenner says AGI by 2027. Futurist Ray Kurzweil bets on 2029 for AI surpassing human intelligence. OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever declared that language models are “slightly conscious.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Notre Dame se apunta un gran tanto con la actualización del estadio a Wi-Fi 6E
item.description: Cuando 80.000 aficionados llenan el emblemático estadio de fútbol de la Universidad de Notre Dame, Indiana (EE.UU), los sábados por la tarde en otoño para animar a los Fighting Irish, no faltan los gritos a todo volumen.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Gcore Radar report reveals 56% year-on-year increase in DDoS attacks
item.description: Gcore, the global edge AI, cloud, network, and security solutions provider, today announced the findings of its Q3-Q4 2024 Radar report into DDoS attack trends.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nokia is bringing ridiculously fast 50Gbps broadband to the UK as the global race for hyperfast internet heats up
item.description: Nokia and Openreach bring ultra-fast 50Gbps broadband to the UK, setting the stage for next-generation internet speeds.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: NASA astronauts are streaming live on Twitch from space. Here’s how to watch
item.description: Ever wondered what life is like for an astronaut? Now you can ask during NASA’s first Twitch event, where astronauts will be broadcasting live from the International Space Station (ISS). The stream is set to take place on Wednesday, February 12, at 11:45 a.m. ET on NASA’s official Twitch channel.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: C++, Go, and Rust gaining popularity – Tiobe
item.description: With Tiobe’s February 2025 rankings, the publisher of the monthly index of programming language popularity notes that fast programming languages such as C++, Go, and Rust are gaining ground.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Internet upgrade part of a move towards 400/800G connectivity
item.description: The industry transition to 400 Gigabit Ethernet networking took a big step forward this week when the world’s leading Internet exchange operator announced plans to upgrade its New York backbone to 400G.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: White-collar jobs are increasingly being exposed to AI
item.description: Report claims 70% of 22,000 tasks could be “transformed” or even “replaced” by AI, but we need better guidance.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Tech unemployment jumps, even as job openings increase
item.description: The unemployment rate among tech workers leaped almost a full percentage point from December to January, according to an analysis of US Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) data released Friday.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Low-code and AI could give businesses the boost they need
item.description: Many businesses already use low-code, but artificial intelligence could make that even more effective.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Marvel debuts new Thunderbolts* trailer during Super Bowl LIX, and it reveals the MCU movie's main villain who you probably expected to be a good guy
item.description: The official trailer for Marvel's Thunderbolts* movie teases plenty of action, jokes, and its main villain.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: La mitad de los líderes de la alta dirección abandonarán sus empresas en dos años
item.description: Una nueva encuesta de Gartner realizada a más de 200 directivos revela que el 56% de estos ve “extremadamente probable” renunciar a su trabajo actual en los próximos dos años, y el 27% planea hacerlo en los próximos seis meses.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Trump's AI ambition and China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit in Paris
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The geopolitics of artificial intelligence will be in focus at a major summit in France where world leaders, executives and experts will hammer out pledges on guiding the development of the rapidly advancing technology.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said he is not interested in acquiring the video-sharing app TikTok, and has no plans regarding what he would do if he owned it. Musk made the remarks during a virtual session at the WELT Economic Summit held on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: Bill would ban DeepSeek
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amazon Agrees to Pay $4 Million Over Tip Subsidies for Delivery Drivers
item.description: Amazon settles with Washington D.C. over allegations it took tips from customers meant for drivers, agreeing to pay nearly $4 million in penalties and fees.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Musk’s crew descends on CFPB with plan to shut it down
item.description: The CFPB is funded independently through the Federal Reserve, but the CFPB director has the power to determine how much money the agency needs. That means a CFPB director could zero out the agency's budget entirely.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg News, The Mercury News

item.title: Bay Area tech job cuts top 2,000 mark in 2025 as sector streamlines
item.description: Several tech companies have revealed plans for a new round of Bay Area layoffs that could erase thousands of jobs
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden
item.description: Some regulations impose compliance or administrative costs/burdens that have no relation to the work to be performed under a government contract. The post Government Procurement Efficiency List: Reducing Regulatory Burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Roger Waldron, Federal News Network

item.title: Network jobs watch: Hiring, skills and certification trends
item.description: Network and infrastructure roles continue to shift as enterprises adopt technologies such as AI-driven network operations, multicloud networking, zero trust network access (ZTNA), and SD-WAN.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: LG recalls 500K electric ovens after fires, pet deaths
item.description: According to the recall, the front-mounted knobs on the recalled ranges can be accidentally activated by humans or pets.
item.dc:creator: Brady Doran, NewsNation

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Elon Musk's lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire's request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company. U.S.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY and MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: DHS research in airport passenger screening never stops
item.description: "We use millimeter technology to collect images of passengers to ensure that no threats that are not allowed on the plane," said John Fortune The post DHS research in airport passenger screening never stops first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network
